data.body: They are native to the MOUNTAINS. A tiny urban back yard at sea level is an incredibly irresponsible, even dangerous place to plant one. (Like many alpine plants, they have shallow roots meant to cling to rocky soil+ will blow over--potentially into your house--in saturated garden soil.)

Things are quite bad in global and personal spheres, but for the last 14 weeks in a row I have lifted weights at least once (usually three times) a week, and I think that's pretty neat.
